questions.question: What is a scalar value lambda called if there exists a non-zero vector v such that A*v = lambda*v for a square matrix A?
If a square matrix has a determinant equal to exactly 0, what can be definitively said about the matrix?
What operation combines the diagonal elements of a square matrix by summing them up?
What does the Rank-Nullity Theorem state for a linear transformation represented by an m x n matrix?
Which process is used to orthogonalize a set of vectors in an inner product space?
If two vectors have an inner (dot) product equal to zero, what is their geometric relationship?
What type of square matrix has the property that its transpose is also equal to its inverse?
What is the dimension of the vector space spanned by the columns of a matrix called?
Which decomposition splits a matrix into an orthogonal matrix, a diagonal matrix of singular values, and another orthogonal matrix?
What is the geometric interpretation of the absolute value of the determinant of a 2x2 matrix?
answers: The area of the parallelogram formed by the column vectors
The angle between the column vectors
The perimeter of the matrix bounds
questions.correctAnswer: The area of the parallelogram formed by the column vectors
